reasoning: Holding presidential elections in Ukraine is heavily constrained by martial law, which constitutionally prohibits elections. There is broad political and public consensus within Ukraine to delay elections until the war ends. Given the protracted nature of the conflict and the immense logistical challenges (displaced citizens, soldiers on the front line, Russian occupation), it is highly improbable that martial law will be lifted or the constitution amended to allow an election before July 1, 2025. Without martial law being lifted, an official election date will almost certainly not be announced.
